title: Heroes4 HTS — MFC Owner-Drawn Controls: 3-Tier Fallback + OCR
desc: Heroes4 PC HTS uses MFC owner-drawn controls with almost no UIA patterns. UIA tier fails silently; Win32 PostMessage is the primary path; SimpleOCR triple cross-validation handles price/candle data.
steps: Tier 1: UIA Invoke/Value/Toggle — attempt first, but owner-drawn controls almost never expose UIA patterns. Expect failure; treat success as a bonus.
Tier 2: Win32 PostMessage — WM_LBUTTONDOWN/UP for clicks, WM_CHAR for text input. This is the primary reliable path for MFC controls.
Tier 3: SimpleOCR — for screen-rendered price/candle data that has no control backing. Apply FuzzyDigitMatch (Levenshtein ≤2) to handle OCR instability on bitmapped fonts.
CMaskEditEx rule: use WM_CHAR (Method 10) ONLY. Never WM_SETTEXT or EM_REPLACESEL — they double-input or get blocked by UIPI on admin-launched processes.
GetSupportedPatterns() forbidden on MFC: calling it triggers COM state corruption. Use TryGetCurrentPattern() per pattern individually. See: uia-getsupportedpatterns-forbidden
author: claude
